title: Providers
intro: Manage generic OpenAI-compatible / Responses-passthrough providers from providers.json. Changes require a restart of mimo2codex for the runtime registry to reload. See <1>generic-providers docs for field details.

file.title: File location
file.notCreated: not created yet — will be created on save
file.currentError: Current file has issues: {{error}} (you can still overwrite from the UI)

table.empty: No generic providers yet. Click '+ Add Provider' to start.

saved.withRestart: Saved to {{path}} — restart mimo2codex to apply

fields.idHint: Letters / digits / - / _ only; must not conflict with builtin mimo / deepseek
fields.shortcutHint: CLI uses --model <shortcut> to switch the default provider
fields.envKeyHint: Which environment variable holds the API key
fields.forceParallelToolCallsSub: Allow multiple tool calls per turn — helps agentic flows
fields.webSearchSub: Only meaningful for upstreams with builtin web_search
fields.strictCompatHint: Sanitizers for strict OpenAI-compatible upstreams that reject non-standard fields. MiniMax is the first beneficiary; the switches also apply to any other strict gateway (private deployments, China-hosted LLM gateways, etc.) that rejects strict:null, content:null, stream_options, multiple system messages, and similar. All off by default — existing providers are unaffected.
fields.minimaxCompatSub: Enables 5 switches by default: dropNullStrict / dropNullContent / dropToolChoiceAuto / mergeSystemMessages / extractThinkTags. dropStreamOptions / dropParallelToolCalls are OpenAI standard fields and are NOT included in the preset — enable them individually only if the upstream actually rejects them
fields.forceDefaultModelSub: When models is empty, rewrite any unknown client model id (e.g. "gpt-5.5") to this provider's defaultModel. Required for MiniMax env-var single-instance; useful for other strict upstreams too
fields.strictCompatSubswitches: Fine-grained switches (the preset already enables 5 of these; listed here so you can toggle individually or enable the two not included in the preset):
fields.dropNullContentSub: Strip assistant content === null field (included in preset)
fields.dropToolChoiceAutoSub: Strip tool_choice === "auto" — auto is the default value (included in preset)
fields.dropStreamOptionsSub: Strip stream_options. ⚠️ Upstream stops returning usage → admin DB token / cache-hit chart go to 0. NOT in preset — enable only if upstream actually 400s on it
fields.dropParallelToolCallsSub: Strip parallel_tool_calls. OpenAI standard field, NOT in preset — enable only when upstream explicitly rejects it
fields.mergeSystemMessagesSub: Merge multiple role:"system" messages into one leading entry (included in preset)
fields.extractThinkTagsSub: Response side: extract <think>...</think> blocks from content into reasoning_content so they don't leak as plain assistant text in Codex (required for MiniMax M1/M2/M3, some GLM/Qwen-thinking models; included in preset)
fields.docsUrlHint: Optional — shown in the 'missing key' error as a link

models.title: Declared models (optional)
models.hint: Leave empty for passthrough — any model id the client sends is forwarded. If you do declare models, only ids (and aliases) listed here will be routed to this provider via byClientModel; declaring contextWindow / maxOutputTokens lets print-config emit accurate toml fields.

validate.idReserved: id "{{id}}" conflicts with a builtin provider — pick another
validate.idFormat: id "{{id}}" must start with a letter/digit; only letters, digits, - and _ are allowed (no spaces)
validate.idDup: A provider with id "{{id}}" already exists — pick another

rawJson.title: Edit raw JSON
rawJson.hint: Edit providers.json verbatim. Save is disabled while JSON is invalid.
rawJson.missingProviders: JSON must have a top-level 'providers' array
